g.text: 3D Data Quality Framework \u00b7 D3 Deflection Engine
State Validator \u2192 Circuit Breaker \u2192 Approval Gate \u2192 Domain Executors \u2192 Feedback Loop
[D3-1] State Validator -- Pre-flight Checks Before Acting
Validates that the system is safe to act:
\u2022 Is the target table still online?
\u2022 Is the anomaly still active (not self-healed)?
\u2022 Has another executor already fixed this anomaly?
\u2022 Is there an active data contract for this table?
Also loads evidence_bundle (job log, DESCRIBE HISTORY,
runbooks, code, prior ITSM) to give downstream agents
Prevents duplicate remediation and race conditions.
A) Execute immediately (auto-fix eligible, LOW blast radius)
B) Require Approval Gate (HIGH blast radius or PII table)
C) Emergency disable rule (runaway rule, high false-positive rate)
D) Hard stop (CRITICAL anomaly + contract breach -- pause all jobs)
\u2022 CRITICAL + contract SLA breached \u2192 Hard stop + escalate
[Auto-fix: blast radius low + PII safe + FP rate ok]
[Approval required: high blast radius / PII / manual]
[D3-2b] Approval Gate -- Owner Sign-off for High-Risk Fixes
Send approval request to Owner + Tech Lead
Evidence: job log + Delta history + runbook
Emergency disable applied -- no further action
Domain Executors are parameterized workflow units,
\u2022 DTC Sales -- order / cart data fixes
\u2022 NPS / Affiliates -- survey / partner data fixes
All executors share the same contract:
Domain routing is determined by data_product_config.
- code_refs -> source file + commit for context
Execute each remediation step in order:
Escalate to Owner + Tech Lead + DataOps
[D3-4] Feedback Loop -- Learnings Refine D1 Baselines
After each resolved anomaly, D3 feeds learnings back to D1 Baseline Learner.
This closes the continuous improvement loop:
D3 resolution data \u2192 D1 Baseline Learner \u2192 refined detection thresholds
Adjust dynamic threshold if drift detected:
D3 Deflection produces fully-closed anomaly records in DIGITAL_DATA_ANOMALIES:
\u2502 evidence_bundle : job log + Delta history + runbook ref \u2502
\u2502 GitHub code ref + prior ITSM fix reused \u2502
